name: Red Norvo
abstract: Red Norvo (born Kenneth Norville; March 31, 1908 – April 6, 1999) was an American musician, one of jazz's early vibraphonists, known as "Mr. Swing".

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (David Torkanowsky) is the subject of the story told by George Wein. George Wein states: 'one of Ellis’s protégés is a young man named David Torkanowsky... I’ve heard him play over the years'. Red Norvo is mentioned only as someone George Wein played with in the past ('Many years ago
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: So eventually I went with Red Norvo. We rehearsed for three months... So Red Norvo and Mildred Bailey came in one night and he said, “I’m starting a band do you want to come with us?” So I said sure.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: the quintet of Red Norvo, Morty Feld, Sid Weiss on bass, Morty Feld on drums, Red Norvo and Goodman and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: I’d been playing with Red Norvo for three years, with the quintet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: And whilst I was up there to visit this girl, at the Colonial Tavern... Red Norvo was playing with his trio... And so I met him and they invited me to go down to Rochester with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: I went there and it was Red Norvo and Mildred Bailey. And I sat in and first thing you know, I didn't know it was being recorded. We were making a record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Palmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 24, 1997
